items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This regularly scheduled security update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation.

items.title: File Upload Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.6) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to privilege escalation.

items.title: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.1) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution due to improper encoding or escaping of output.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability (CVSS 9.8) in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store using a polyglot technique. Adobe released Magento 2.4.9 containing the fix on May 12, 2026, but older supported versions remain vulnerable without specific mitigation.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released APSB26-05, a critical security update addressing multiple vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. These vulnerabilities could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, and execute unauthorized malicious code.
